message:
chore(beads): add 4 more hgjp subtasks for content + primer + test coverage

Third tranche of child beads under the Wills & Estate Planning
article epic. Covers article body content sections, agent-readable
primer markdown, writing-index + command-palette registration, and
the Playwright smoke test. Four beads, same parent-child wiring as
the previous two tranches.

@@ -173,7 +173,18 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","title":"Sample session walkthrough (blended-family HNW archetype with IntakePhasesViz)","description":"The emotional centerpiece. A narrated sample session that makes the 9 intake phases concrete.

## Archetype chosen
A **Tier 3 HNW blended family** — because it hits the most instructive patterns in one session:
- Second marriage (both spouses have first-marriage kids)
- One adult child from first marriage is in active addiction recovery
- Primary residence in NY (state estate tax!), vacation home in FL
- One spouse has a pre-IPO startup stake (concentrated position + liquidity risk)
- Family parrot (longer life expectancy than many humans — ties back to the hero illustration and the X-post hook)
- Modest crypto self-custody
- Previous will from 2018 (pre-OBBBA, pre-Secure Act, pre-remarriage)

This archetype cleanly triggers: elective-share, QTIP, vulnerable-heir SNT or incentive trust, NY-FL domicile analysis, liquidity-at-death operator, digital-asset inventory, pet-trust.

## Section structure (id="intake")
SectionHeader eyebrow="A sample session" title="What it actually feels like."

### Presentation
- Narrated from the users perspective ("Maya, 54, remarrying for the second time").
- 9 phases laid out as scenes. For each phase, show:
  - The agents conversational prompt (plain English, not forms).
  - Mayas answer (excerpted / paraphrased).
  - The artifact the agent quietly produces (e.g., `intake-record.md` appended).
  - Any landmine the agent flags as it goes (e.g., "Your ex-husband is still the primary beneficiary on the old 401(k). Flagging `red-flag-triage.md`.").
- After scene 5 (family dynamics), the agent triggers the `blended-family` overlay and the tone of the questions deepens.

### Embed IntakePhasesViz
Visualization showing all 9 phases as a horizontal flow (Orientation → People → Assets → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Tier Routing). Each node:
- Clickable — drills into what that phase asks
- Highlight when the narrative is "in" that phase
- Shows approximate time spent (5/10/15 min as in SKILL.md)

### Close the scene
- End with Maya getting a Comprehensive Plan Report, an Attorney Engagement Brief, and a 30-item Implementation Ledger — all produced across a weekend, rather than six months of attorney email threads.
- Crucial line: "The attorney she eventually hires doesnt look at this packet and say a robot did your planning. She looks at it and says good — youve done my diligence work. I can draft in three hours instead of fifteen, and Ill charge you for three."

## Why this archetype
The slack articles emotional centerpiece was the cutover moment. The wills article needs an equivalent — and the blended-family + addicted-child + parrot + crypto combo is vivid enough to stick. Avoid making the archetype so rich that no reader identifies with it; keep one or two elements (the remarriage, the kids) universal.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads like narrative, not documentation.
- Every `<J>` tooltip works.
- IntakePhasesViz phase-linking animates smoothly as the reader scrolls through the scenes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.15","title":"What the skill produces: deliverables section + DeliverablesTreeViz","description":"Concrete deliverables inventory section — tangible proof that "200 files" is real work, not marketing.

## Section structure (id="produces")
SectionHeader eyebrow="The output" title="The 45 artifacts you walk away with."

### Intro
Every serious session produces a structured project directory, not a pile of loose files. The skill enforces a coverage matrix (`analyses/plan-coverage-matrix.md`) and runs `scripts/plan-validator.py` as a backstop to catch untouched starter outputs. What you end up with, on a weekend afternoon, is something a law firm would charge $8-25k to produce in a month.

### Embed DeliverablesTreeViz
Interactive folder tree:
- `my-estate-plan/`
  - `intake/` (2 files)
    - `intake-record.md`
    - `session-NN-summary.md`
  - `analyses/` (20 files) — `current-document-audit`, `beneficiary-form-audit`, `titling-audit`, `coherence-audit`, `tax-exposure-analysis`, `liquidity-analysis`, `prior-plan-gap-analysis`, `decision-ledger`, `official-source-log`, `red-flag-triage`, `document-acquisition-plan`, `evidence-confidence-map`, `stress-test-scenarios`, `plan-coverage-matrix`, `document-quality-triage`, `recommendation-confidence-register`, `fiduciary-bench-scorecard`, `litigation-risk-memo`, `attorney-handoff-readiness`, `foreign-and-conflict-of-laws-review`.
  - `deliverables/` (23 files) — `asset-inventory`, `beneficiary-map`, `plan-report`, `implementation-ledger`, `letter-of-instruction`, `digital-inventory`, `personal-property-memorandum`, `letter-of-wishes`, `ethical-will`, `family-meeting-agenda`, `conflict-prevention-plan`, `if-i-die-tomorrow`, `disposition-of-remains`, `executor-checklist`, `attorney-interview-questions`, `attorney-engagement-brief`, `document-package-index`, `review-schedule`, `signing-readiness-checklist`, `funding-proof-log`, `institution-contact-matrix`, `beneficiary-change-packet`, `business-continuity-activation`.

Each leaf:
- Click reveals: a 2-sentence plain-English description + a representative output snippet (from the skills `assets/` templates).
- Color-coded by type: intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald.
- Mode-dependent shading: artifacts only required in some modes (e.g., `business-continuity-activation` only if user owns a business) are marked "mode-dependent" on hover.

### Three spotlight deliverables (pulled out of the tree as prose callouts)
Highlight the three most emotionally and practically important:

1. **`if-i-die-tomorrow.md`** — One page. Where every document lives. Who to call first. Passwords pointer. This is the page your spouse or executor actually uses in the first 48 hours. Everything else is for later.
2. **`beneficiary-map.md`** — Every account, every contract, who is currently named, who should be named, and the delta. This is the single artifact that catches 80% of real-world plan failures before they happen.
3. **`attorney-engagement-brief.md`** — The hand-off packet. Your lawyer goes from "let me read your whole life" to "heres what I need to draft" in one sitting.

### Honest caveat
The skill does not sign documents, file them with the county, or execute them. Every path ends at an attorney. The artifacts make that attorneys job smaller, not optional.

## Acceptance criteria
- DeliverablesTreeViz mounts and is navigable via keyboard.
- Filter toggle (intake / analyses / deliverables / all) works.
- Spotlight callouts are visually distinct from the tree.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:06.928844820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:06.928844820Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.15","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:06.928844820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","title":"9-step workflow narrative section","description":"Section showing the 9-step end-to-end workflow as an explicit flow — counterweight to the intake section (which is 1-on-1 dialogue). This one is the skills macro structure.

## Section structure (id="workflow")
SectionHeader eyebrow="End-to-end" title="The 9 steps, in order."

### Content
Walk through the 9 steps from SKILL.md, each with a 2-3 sentence description in plain English:

1. **Orient** — Why now? What triggered this? (A move, a birth, a divorce, a diagnosis, a scare.) Set the disclaimer; confirm "this is not legal advice."
2. **Inventory** — People + assets + beneficiary audit. Produce the Asset Inventory, Beneficiary Map, Evidence Confidence Map.
3. **Surface complications** — Blended family? Addiction? Disability? Ex-spouse still on a form? This is where the agent names the landmines.
4. **Clarify goals** — Equal vs. equitable. Charitable intent. Business succession. Write these in your own words (they go into the letter of wishes later).
5. **Design structure** — Apply the kernel + operators + tier logic. Pick will + trust + POA architecture. Name executor, trustee, guardian, agents.
6. **Incapacity & end-of-life** — Durable POA, healthcare proxy, living will, HIPAA, POLST if appropriate, disposition of remains.
7. **Tax & domicile** — Federal exemption + state estate / inheritance tax + portability + basis planning. Domicile audit if multi-state.
8. **Communication & legacy** — Family meeting agenda, letter of wishes, ethical will, digital inventory, conflict-prevention plan, "if I die tomorrow" package.
9. **Attorney handoff** — The Comprehensive Plan Report + Attorney Engagement Brief + Interview Questions. Execution checklist. Review cadence.

### Visualization idea (may piggyback on IntakePhasesViz)
If the viz in the sample-session section is already designed to show *phases* (the conversational side), this workflow section could use a simpler horizontal step-bar indicator, or we reuse the same component in a different mode. Decide during implementation; avoid shipping two redundant flow visualizations.

### Key line to land
"Intake (the 9 phases) is what you feel while using the skill. The workflow (the 9 steps) is what the agent and the skill produce for you in parallel. By the end of phase 9, step 9 is also done, and you hand the whole packet to a lawyer."

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads as a natural follow-up to the sample-session section.
- Doesnt repeat the intake-phases content verbatim; steps focus on *what the agent produces*, phases focused on *what the user experiences*.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:26.404424657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:26.404424657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:26.404424657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.17","title":"Anti-patterns section with AntiPatternCardsViz (parrot / ex-spouse / springing POA / etc.)","description":"The section that makes the stakes vivid. Each anti-pattern is a "you would not have thought of this on your own" moment.

## Section structure (id="anti")
SectionHeader eyebrow="What goes wrong" title="Anti-patterns: the parrot, the ex-spouse, the springing POA."

### Intro
Most estate-plan disasters are not exotic. Theyre one of ~15 patterns the skill explicitly scans for. Here are the ones worth burning into your memory before you do anything else.

### Embed AntiPatternCardsViz
A grid of flip-cards. Front: the catchy pattern name. Back: what goes wrong, how the skill catches it, and which subagent flags it.

### Must-include cards (minimum 10, curated for drama + instructiveness)

1. **The ex-spouse still on the 401(k).** The classic. Your will says "to my kids." Your plan administrator mails the check to your ex anyway, because ERISA + beneficiary designation overrides the will. Flagged by `beneficiary-audit` subagent in Phase 4 of intake.
2. **Funding a revocable trust on paper but never re-titling assets.** The trust exists. No assets are in it. The "pour-over will" still sends things through probate. Flagged by `funding-checklist-generator`.
3. **Putting the crypto seed phrase in the will.** Wills become public record at probate. You just published your seed phrase. Use a separate digital inventory.
4. **Springing POA that requires a physician certification.** Banks refuse to honor them in practice because "is this person incapacitated" is a scary question to answer. Use immediate durable POA with a trusted agent.
5. **Co-executors in a conflicted family.** Guarantees gridlock. The skills `fiduciary-bench-builder` will try to talk you out of it.
6. **Naming minors directly as beneficiaries.** Forces the money through court-supervised property management or dumps it on them at 18. Use a trust or an UTMA with a deliberate release age.
7. **"Everything to my spouse, trust them to take care of my kids" in a blended family.** The spouse can rewrite their will after you die and disinherit the first-marriage children. Use a QTIP.
8. **The pet trust you forgot about.** Your parrot outlives you. You never named a caretaker. The parrot ends up in a shelter or, yes, at the taxidermist. Use a pet trust with a named caretaker and a funded trustee.
9. **Failing to file Form 706 for portability after the first spouses death.** You lose the DSUE — sometimes millions in future exemption. One-time filing; easy to miss without the skills nudge.
10. **Signing a will in a state with witness rules you didnt follow.** Holographic wills in states that barely recognize them are a litigation magnet. The `execution-formalities-router` subagent prevents this.
11. **Addiction + outright distribution.** Your adult child is in recovery. Leaving them $400k outright is a plausible way to destabilize them and kill them. Use an incentive trust with a responsible trustee and clear anti-enabling clauses.
12. **NRA spouse + $60,000 exemption trap.** Non-resident-alien spouse owning U.S. assets has a vastly lower exemption without planning. QDOT + treaty analysis required.
13. **Updating documents a week before death.** Classic undue-influence setup. Looks like the dying person was coerced by the caregiver who was around most. Avoid.

### Closing paragraph
"The skill cannot make you rich or remove grief from dying. What it can do is make sure none of the above patterns are live in your plan on the day they would matter."

## Acceptance criteria
- 10+ cards rendered.
- Each flip interaction is accessible (keyboard + screen reader).
- Cards "what the skill catches this with" line correctly names the subagent.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:57.118611189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:57.118611189Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.17","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:57.118611189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.18","title":"Verification discipline + attorney handoff section","description":"Trust and credibility section. The reader has to believe the skill knows what it doesnt know, and that it ends at a real attorney.

## Section structure (id="attorney")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Handing off" title="The attorney still signs the documents."

### Two sub-sections

#### A. Verification-first discipline
- Estate-planning law is volatile and state-specific. Federal thresholds shift (OBBBA just set it to $15M per person for 2026). State estate tax thresholds change. Execution-formality rules vary state by state. Secure Act, Secure 2.0, and OBBBA have each rewritten parts of the rules in the last few years.
- The skill separates **evergreen methodology** (the kernel, operators, tier routing) from **volatile law** (thresholds, formalities, portability details). Every live-law check must be verified against primary sources (IRS, state tax department, state judiciary, secretary of state, state health department) and logged in `analyses/official-source-log.md`.
- Mandatory verification triggers include: federal transfer-tax thresholds, state estate/inheritance thresholds, execution formalities, elective share, community property, TOD/Lady Bird deed availability, Medicaid lookback, POLST/MOLST naming, NFA transfer rules.
- This isnt scrupulous overkill; its how you prevent an agent from cheerfully recommending a strategy that was correct in 2022 and is now malpractice-adjacent.

#### B. The attorney handoff packet
- The skill ends at, not replaces, a licensed estate-planning attorney in the users state. Every session produces an **Attorney Engagement Brief** + **Attorney Interview Questions** + **Document Package Index** + **Attorney Handoff Readiness** scorecard.
- The point of the handoff packet is to turn the attorney from "first, let me understand your life" into "good, I can draft this." Typical first-meeting billable time drops from ~8-15 hours to ~2-4 hours.
- The Attorney Handoff Readiness scorecard is a self-audit — before you actually send the packet, the skill grades whether counsel can draft efficiently from what youve produced. If it comes back red, you do another pass before the meeting.

### Why this section matters legally + reputationally
The skill cannot and must not be positioned as "AI replaces lawyers." That framing is:
- Legally dangerous for users (unauthorized practice of law, missed state-specific rules).
- Legally dangerous for jeffreys-skills.md (potential UPL enforcement).
- Empirically wrong (the judgment layer is what estate planners get paid for; the draft layer is what the skill handles).

The article must land the reverse framing: "AI gives you a tractable, better-informed *first meeting* with a real attorney, and produces the materials that meeting needs."

### Closing line
Something like: "The skills job is to hand your attorney the best packet theyve ever received from a new client. Theirs is to sign the documents. Thats the whole thing."

## Acceptance criteria
- Disclaimer explicit and non-weaselly.
- Reader leaves with clear mental model: skill produces audit + drafts, attorney signs.
- `verification-first` concept is named and explained in one paragraph.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:21.004323702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:21.004323702Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.18","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:21.004323702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","title":"TL;DR / pattern generalization section","description":"Closing section that mirrors the slack articles "A pattern, not a migration" — zooming out from wills-specifically to the broader pattern.

## Section structure (id="pattern")
SectionHeader (no eyebrow) title="A pattern, not a product."

### Content
- Estate planning is one of a small but growing set of "expensive-by-default" life-admin domains where the judgment layer is abundant in the world (good attorneys exist) but expensive to *reach* (you have to identify them, interview them, book them, pay them by the hour, wait for their drafts, review them, go back and forth), and where most of the friction is in asking good questions, listening, and writing things down. Thats the exact shape of a problem an agent-plus-skill can dramatically compress.
- Tax preparation was the first skill on jeffreys-skills.md to hit this pattern. Several readers saved $1,000+, one saved $20,000, on a $20/month subscription. Wills is now the second.
- The pattern generalizes: Medicare-plan selection, Social-Security-claiming strategy, college-aid-optimization, divorce-financial-planning, medical-bill-auditing, long-term-care-insurance. Each is "an expensive specialist asking good questions for 8-15 hours." Each is a plausible next skill.
- But — and this is the genuinely important sentence — **none of these compress to zero**. The attorney still signs. The CPA still files. The Medicare broker still enrolls you. The agent-plus-skill pattern moves most of the *preparation* out of the specialists billable hour and into your Saturday afternoon, at 1/1000th the cost. The professional still does the professional part. Its the preparation that was secretly ~80% of the bill.

### Sign-off card (pattern matches slack article)
TL;DR card with: "Two hundred files. One weekend. Your attorney signs. Your spouse can find the 401(k) beneficiary form. Your parrot is provided for."

## Acceptance criteria
- Closes the article on a forward-looking note without over-claiming.
- Links back to jsm.md dashboard + the tax-preparation skill as the "now try the other one" CTA.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:38.950713893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:38.950713893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:38.950713893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","title":"Commission/generate hero illustration (assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp)","description":"Create the hero illustration for the article, paralleling `assets/slack_migration_post_illustration.webp`.

## Concept
A warm, editorial scene — a kitchen table under soft lamplight with a stack of important documents (will, deed, 401(k) form, a crypto hardware wallet, a passport), a laptop open showing an agent chat window, and a small glowing parrot perched on a chair back. The parrot is the visual anchor: it references Jeffreys X post line about "your beloved parrot that has another 20 years of life remaining." Warm purple/amber/emerald glow, same palette as the slack illustration.

## Workflow
1. Generate candidates via the preferred pipeline (likely Midjourney/SDXL/etc. via Jeffreys existing image-gen tooling).
2. Pick one, upscale, convert to WebP at ~560px wide display size (with 2x source for retina).
3. Place at `assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp`.
4. Reference from the article via `import illustration from "@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp"`.

## Acceptance criteria
- WebP file exists at the expected path.
- Used as the hero image via Next `<Image priority placeholder="blur">` pattern.
- Alt text is descriptive: "Warmly-lit desk scene with estate-planning documents, a laptop running an AI agent, and a parrot — evoking an estate plan drafted at home with the help of an AI skill."
- File size under ~700 KB (slack hero is 646 KB).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","title":"Scaffold components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx` — "use client" module that exports all five named visualizations the article dynamic-imports.

## Scaffold scope
- `"use client"` directive.
- Imports: React (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`, `useMemo`), framer-motion (`motion`, `AnimatePresence`, `useInView`, `useReducedMotion`), lucide-react icons, maybe `@react-three/fiber` or similar for 3D if any viz warrants it (probably not — 2D SVG/divs will suffice).
- Shared hooks: reduced-motion guard, mobile breakpoint detector (mirrors hooks already used in this repo).
- Five named exports (skeletons for now, implemented in downstream beads):
  - `TierTriageViz` — 5-rung wealth ladder
  - `AxiomCoherenceViz` — 8-spoke coherence diagram with mismatch toggle
  - `IntakePhasesViz` — 9-phase horizontal flow
  - `DeliverablesTreeViz` — 45-leaf deliverables tree
  - `AntiPatternCardsViz` — flip-card grid

## Why a single file
- Dynamic-imported with `{ ssr: false }` — wants to be one module for bundler locality.
- Mirrors slack articles pattern (`slack-migration-visualizations.tsx` is ~2500 lines with 4 visualizations).
- AGENTS.md discourages file sprawl. Keep everything article-specific in one viz module.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, all five named exports stubbed with placeholder `<div>` each so the article component can import them without runtime error.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` clean.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:53.483882643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:53.483882643Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:53.483882643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","title":"TierTriageViz: interactive 5-tier wealth ladder","description":"Implement `TierTriageViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Vertical (mobile) / horizontal (desktop) ladder with 5 rungs.
- Each rung: title + net-worth band (e.g., "Tier 1 · Modest · < $500K"), archetype thumbnail, one-line goal summary.
- Default-selected tier: Tier 2 (middle-class) — because its the modal reader.
- Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel with:
  - Archetype description (renter → dynasty)
  - Primary goals (guardianship → transfer-tax minimization)
  - Core deliverables (will + POAs → GRAT + IDGT + family constitution)
  - Typical complications (blended family, non-citizen spouse, business interests, vulnerable heirs)
- Active-rung accent color matches the design systems purple/cyan/emerald/amber/rose palette.

## Data
Pull the tier table verbatim from SKILL.mds "Wealth-Tier Triage" section:
- Tier 1 — Modest — < $500K — Young family, no business — Guardianship, basic will — Will, POA, healthcare directive, beneficiary audit
- Tier 2 — Middle-Class — $500K-$3M — Home + 401k + kids — Probate avoidance — + Revocable trust, TOD deed, pour-over will, 529 continuity
- Tier 3 — HNW — $3M-$15M — Home + investments + business + state-tax exposure — State estate tax, blended family, business succession — + QTIP/CST, ILIT, buy-sell, domicile planning
- Tier 4 — UHNW — $15M-$100M — Multi-asset, multi-state — Federal estate tax, GST, asset protection — + GRAT, IDGT, SLAT, dynasty trust, §6166, FLP
- Tier 5 — Industrialist — $100M+ — Operating empire, multi-generation, philanthropy — Multi-gen transfer, governance, cross-border — + Family office, private foundation, CLT/CRT, offshore, family constitution

## Complexity overlay
Below the ladder, a compact list of "what bumps you up" (blended family +1, non-citizen spouse +1, private business +1, vulnerable heir +1, multi-state/country +1, NFA firearms → specialty, self-custody crypto → specialty, etc.). Implement as a small chip row that highlights the current tier + overlay impact.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys walk rungs, Enter to expand.
- Touch: tap to expand, tap-again to collapse.
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no transitions, instant state change.

## Acceptance criteria
- Renders at all viewport sizes.
- Active-rung detail panel describes the tier accurately per SKILL.md.
- Fully keyboard-navigable and screen-reader-friendly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:14.352525529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:14.352525529Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:14.352525529Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","title":"AxiomCoherenceViz: 8-spoke diagram with mismatch mode","description":"Implement `AxiomCoherenceViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Radial/hub-and-spoke SVG diagram.
- Center node: "You" (your domicile + your plan).
- 8 spokes to asset / decision categories:
  1. Primary residence (house)
  2. 401(k) / employer retirement
  3. IRAs
  4. Life insurance
  5. Taxable brokerage
  6. Financial POAs
  7. Healthcare POAs + living will + HIPAA
  8. Digital inventory (email, password manager, crypto)
- Each spoke terminates at a small card showing "what document controls this" (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA).

## The toggle
A pill at the top: **[Coherent] | [Incoherent]** — two preset states.

- **Coherent mode**: every spoke has a green check; the documents align.
- **Incoherent mode**: 3 spokes turn red with flashlight overlay:
  - 401(k) still names ex-spouse (Axiom 3 override).
  - House is joint-tenancy with an ex-sibling (Axiom 7, titling overrides will).
  - Crypto wallet has no seed-phrase pointer (Axiom 0 adjacent, but unrecoverable category).

## Interactions
- Click a spoke → inline paragraph beneath the viz explains what went wrong and which subagent catches it.
- Toggle: smoothly animates arrows + colors.
- Keyboard: tab through spokes, Enter to open detail.

## Why this viz
The whole core-insight section hinges on the reader *seeing* that coherence is the thing. A still-image diagram would be okay; an interactive toggle that literally shows the plan breaking is memorable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Mounts without SSR errors.
- Reduced-motion respected.
- Both modes are fully explorable (not just eye candy).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:28.844049716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:28.844049716Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:28.844049716Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","title":"IntakePhasesViz: 9-phase flow with drill-in","description":"Implement `IntakePhasesViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Horizontal flow on desktop; vertical stack on mobile.
- 9 nodes: Orientation → People → Assets & Liabilities → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Wealth-Tier Routing.
- Each node shows:
  - Phase number + name
  - Approximate time (5 / 10 / 15 min per SKILL.md)
  - An icon from lucide-react
- Connected by arrows. Active phase highlighted.

## Scroll-linked highlighting
The articles sample-session section walks through a narrative that enters each phase in order. Use IntersectionObserver on paragraph markers to highlight the current phase as the reader scrolls.

## Click-to-drill
Click any node → inline panel under the viz shows:
- The agents opening question for that phase (plain English)
- The typical artifact written in that phase (e.g., `intake-record.md` updated, `red-flag-triage.md` created)
- Branch points (e.g., "if user says blended family, trigger overlay")

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys to walk, Enter to drill.
- Touch: tap to drill; the scroll-link still highlights the phase the reader is "in."
- Reduced motion: no arrow animations; still highlights correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scroll-link works smoothly and doesnt flicker.
- Drill-in copy is accurate to SKILL.md section "The 9 Intake Phases."
- Responsive at 320px through 1440px.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","title":"DeliverablesTreeViz: 45-leaf interactive folder tree","description":"Implement `DeliverablesTreeViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Collapsible folder tree: top-level `my-estate-plan/` → three child folders `intake/`, `analyses/`, `deliverables/`.
- Leaf count: 2 + 20 + 23 = 45 files (exact match to the output contract in SKILL.md).
- Files represented as subtle pill rows with filename, icon, and tier-color (intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald).
- Mode-dependent files show a small "mode:" tag on hover (e.g., `business-continuity-activation.md` shows `mode: business-owner-succession`).

## Filters (toolbar above tree)
- **Show only**: All / Intake / Analyses / Deliverables.
- **Mode filter**: All / new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review.
- **Search input**: matches filename or plain-English purpose.

## Click-a-leaf behavior
Opens an inline detail card below the tree with:
- Filename
- 2-sentence plain-English purpose
- Representative snippet (2-5 lines) adapted from the template in `assets/` (lightly paraphrased for the web; we do not publish the full templates here — those are behind the paywall).
- Icon link to the relevant section of SKILL.md on the jeffreys-skills.md catalog page.

## Data source
A static const array at the top of the viz file, hand-curated from the bead Im reading now + `assets/` directory listing. Each entry: `{ path, category, purpose, modes[], sampleLines }`. Commit to writing all 45 entries by hand to keep them high-quality; do not scrape.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: tree is a proper `role="tree"` with up/down/left/right.
- Touch: tap to expand/collapse folders; tap leaves to open detail.
- Reduced motion: no expand/collapse animation, snap.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 45 leaves render.
- Filter combinations work (e.g., Mode=executor-activation shows fewer leaves highlighted).
- Detail card accurate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz: 13-card flip grid","description":"Implement `AntiPatternCardsViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Responsive grid (4-col desktop / 2-col tablet / 1-col mobile) of flip-cards.
- ≥13 cards (one per anti-pattern listed in the anti-patterns section bead).
- Front of card: evocative pattern name + one-line hook + emoji/icon.
- Back of card: what actually goes wrong, worst-case outcome, which subagent the skill uses to catch it.

## Interactions
- Hover (desktop) or tap (mobile) to flip. Reduced motion: dissolve instead of 3D flip.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus, Space/Enter to flip.
- Cards are buttons with `aria-pressed` state for screen readers.
- On focus, a subtle ring appears matching the design system accent.

## Data source
Hand-authored array of `{ name, hook, explanation, worstCase, subagent }` objects. Use the 13-card starter list in the anti-patterns section bead.

## Style
Visual weight matches the slack articles callout cards. Each card has a subtle gradient border; hover glow.

## Acceptance criteria
- All cards flip.
- Reading the backs of the 13 cards gives the reader a comprehensive enough picture of the skills risk-scanning layer.
- Accessible: cards should be reachable and readable with keyboard alone.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","title":"OpenGraph + Twitter images (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx, twitter-image.tsx)","description":"Create the two image-route files for the article, modeled on the slack articles equivalents.

## Files
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx`
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image.tsx`

Use Nexts `ImageResponse` (next/og) to generate 1200×630 (OG) and 1200×600 (Twitter summary-large-image) cards at build time.

## Design
Match the visual identity of the slack articles OG card but rebrand for wills:
- Dark background (same `#020204` base).
- Title: "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday."
- Subtitle: "AI agents · paid skill · attorney signs the docs."
- Faint illustration echo (could reuse the parrot motif from the hero, or a cleaner document stack).
- Author badge at bottom-left: "Jeffrey Emanuel · jeffreyemanuel.com".

## Dependencies
- `@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp` (from hero-illustration bead).
- Fonts loaded via the `fetch` pattern used in the existing OG files.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both images generated at build time (`bun run build` produces them).
- URLs render correctly when shared on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and iMessage.
- File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","title":"CSS styles: decide on sm- reuse vs wep- namespace (globals.css)","description":"Audit `app/globals.css` for every `sm-*` class the slack article uses (sm-scope, sm-body, sm-progress-bar, sm-drop-cap, sm-section-title, sm-subheading, sm-subheading-eyebrow, sm-code-block, sm-table-wrap, sm-table, sm-toc, sm-viz-wrap, sm-callout, sm-callout-emerald, sm-insight-card, sm-sign-off, sm-decisions, sm-display-title, sm-fade-section, sm-visible, sm-reveal-ready, etc.).

## Decision
Two options:
- **Reuse `sm-*`**: simplest. Zero new CSS. Works out of the box if the wills article uses the same visual language.
- **Namespace to `wep-*`**: safer if we expect the wills articles visual identity to diverge (e.g., warmer palette, softer shadows). Adds ~800 lines of CSS.

Default: reuse. Only introduce `wep-*` if a section bead requires a visually distinct style that cannot be expressed as a modifier class (e.g., `sm-insight-card sm-insight-card--wep-warm`).

## Action
- Read `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`: list every `sm-` class referenced.
- Run the article through a dry build; confirm no missing classes.
- If any warm-palette variants are needed (e.g., an amber-accented callout for the "if I die tomorrow" deliverable), add them as modifier classes on the existing `sm-` definitions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article renders correctly with the existing stylesheet.
- No CSS duplication unless strictly required.
- Any new additions documented with a brief comment at the top of the new block.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

## Why
The article uses the `<J t="term">` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader.

## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in)
- will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will
- POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST
- beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE
- probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead
- QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust
- step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption
- titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed
- ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act
- guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee
- domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate
- asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause
- NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact
- charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation
- OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance

## Structure per term
```ts
{
  term: "QTIP",
  full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust",
  definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.",
  source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." }  // optional
}
```

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, ~40+ terms defined.
- Imported and used via `<J t="qtip">` inline in the article component.
- Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","title":"Scaffold article component (components/wills-estate-article.tsx)","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-article.tsx` as a "use client" component modeled exactly on `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`.

## Scope of this bead (scaffold only — sections land in follow-up beads)
- Imports: React, Next `Image`, Next fonts (Crimson_Pro, JetBrains_Mono, Bricolage_Grotesque), lucide-react icons (tentative: FileDown, ScrollText, ShieldCheck, HeartPulse, HandCoins, Landmark, Sparkles, BookOpen), `MathTooltip`, `getJargon` from `@/lib/wills-estate-jargon`, `getScrollMetrics` from `@/lib/utils`, and illustration asset.
- Dynamic imports for the five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz) from `./wills-estate-visualizations`, each `{ ssr: false }` because they use browser APIs / framer-motion.
- Font variables set up the same way as slack article (`--font-crimson`, etc).
- Wrapper components defined locally (these can be copy-pasted from slack article with the `sm-` → `wep-` class-name rename if any styles diverge; otherwise keep `sm-` so we share the stylesheet): `J` (jargon tooltip), `EC` (editorial container, 800px centered), `Divider`, `Code`, `Mono`, `SectionHeader`, `Sub`, `RefTable`.
- Primer download button constant: `PRIMER_HREF = "/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md"`, `PRIMER_FILENAME = "WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md"`.
- Main exported component `WillsEstateArticle` with:
  - `scrollProgress` state + scroll listener (copy pattern from slack).
  - IntersectionObserver-driven section reveal (copy pattern from slack, reuses `sm-fade-section` / `sm-visible` classes in globals.css).
  - Outer `div` with ref, role="main", className combines `sm-scope sm-body` + font variable classes, same background `#020204` and text `#f8fafc`.
  - Scroll progress bar rendered at top.
  - Empty section placeholders (stubs with TODO comments) for each section bead to fill in: hero, download-cta, intro, catalog, install, who-this-is-for, core-insight, sample-session, produces, workflow, anti-patterns, verification, tldr.

## Why a single scaffold bead
Every content-section bead will modify this file. Scaffolding it once means downstream section beads can land in parallel without file-creation conflicts. Per AGENTS.md "Backwards Compatibility & File Sprawl" rule: one article component, not ten.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, `bun tsc --noEmit` is clean once the route + jargon beads land too.
- Has placeholder stubs for every section.
- Uses the existing `sm-*` CSS classes until we confirm we need a `wep-*` namespace (decided in the styles bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation:

## 1. `lib/content.ts`
Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include:
- slug: `wills-and-estate-planning`
- title: final article title
- description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card
- date: publication date
- tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]`
- href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
- hero image reference

## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search)
Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered.

## Why this bead is separate
These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article shows up on `/writing`.
- `Cmd+K` → "wills" / "estate" / "planning" surfaces the article.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
